page: Amazon Web Services inf2.24xlarge is a virtualized server instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or discrete GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the inf2.24xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU tests placing it in the top 10% tier. Single-core performance is also strong, ranking in the top 25%. Memory performance is highly capable, highlighted by a top-tier PassMark memory score and strong cached read-only memory bandwidth of 5929.9 GB/sec for small blocks, though uncached large-block read and write speeds fall into the average range. Cryptographic capabilities are notable, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring in the top 10%. For specialized workloads, the instance achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds of 3801.11 tokens/sec using a small 135M LLM model, while text generation speeds for the same model remain average.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the inf2.24xlarge offers a resource-dense profile suitable for compute-heavy and memory-intensive tasks without the premium of specialized memory-optimized hardware. The absence of local storage means workloads must rely on network storage, which is supported by the 50 Gbps network interface. This configuration is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving, as well as specific language model prompt processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services inf2.24xlarge is a Nitro-based virtual server featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors running at 3.6 GHz. It is configured with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps with no local storage or discrete GPUs. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside strong cryptographic and database execution capabilities. It is optimized for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services inf2.24xlarge is a Nitro-based instance with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services inf2.24xlarge features 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth for high-performance multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-core AMD EPYC virtual server delivering balanced memory density, 50 Gbps networking, and top-tier multi-core processing power.

384 GB of DDR4 system memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
